dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: Manage risk assessments in accordance with EN ISO 12100
dashboard.newAnalysisDesc: Generate a PDF report and CE declaration of conformity per the Machinery Directive

analysis.subscriptionDesc: Activate a Pro plan to save analyses and download PDF reports.
analysis.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this analysis? This action is irreversible.
analysis.matrixTitle: Risk matrix S×P — method per EN ISO 12100
analysis.tableHint: S = severity of harm · P = probability · R = S×P
analysis.criticalFull: Machine requires immediate protective measures before operation. Hazards:
analysis.warningFull: Implementation of technical protective measures required.
analysis.acceptableFull: Acceptable with use of personal protective equipment (PPE).
analysis.m1full: — design change eliminating or reducing the hazard (inherently safe design).
analysis.m2full: — guards, barriers, light curtains, emergency stops, electromagnetic interlocks.
analysis.m3full: — ISO 7010 pictograms, work instructions, safety training, PPE.
analysis.disclaimer: ⚠ This analysis is advisory and does not replace consultation with a certified safety professional. The client bears full responsibility for the accuracy of the data.
analysis.pdfPromoDesc: The PDF report includes your company logo and data, ready to submit to clients or regulatory authorities.
analysis.step1Desc: Select a machine category and type — the system will automatically assign standards and hazards
analysis.step2Desc: Machine identification data and client information (will appear on the report)
analysis.step3Desc: S×P method in accordance with EN ISO 12100
analysis.step4Desc: Risk assessment results using S×P method · EN ISO 12100 · Directive 2006/42/EC
analysis.autoThreatsDesc: hazards ready to add with one click
analysis.criticalText: high-level hazards — machine requires immediate protective measures before operation.
analysis.warningText: medium hazards — technical protective measures required.
analysis.acceptableText: low hazards — acceptable with use of PPE.
analysis.m2: — guards, barriers, light curtains, emergency stops.
analysis.residualRiskDesc: Select the effectiveness of each protective measure. • High (P-2): electrical interlocks, light curtains PL d/e. • Medium (P-1): fixed guards, barriers. • Low: signage, training, PPE. Severity (S) does not change.
analysis.machineBoundariesDesc: Per ISO 12100 §5.3 — define spatial, temporal and use limits of the machine.
analysis.intendedUsePlaceholder: Describe machine purpose, materials, operating mode...
analysis.spaceLimitsPlaceholder: Max part dimensions, operator reach, working zone...
analysis.lifecycleSelectLabel: Select lifecycle phases in which this hazard may occur:
analysis.hierarchy1desc: Design change eliminating or reducing the hazard at source
analysis.hierarchy2desc: Guards, light curtains, interlocks, emergency stops, STO
analysis.hierarchy3desc: ISO 7010 pictograms, work instructions, safety training, PPE
analysis.hierarchyAssign: Assign each hazard to the appropriate hierarchy level:
analysis.residualDesc: For each hazard, select measure effectiveness, then accept or enter S' and P' manually.
analysis.plrDesc: For each hazard, select parameter P and provide justification.
analysis.plrP1Desc: avoidance possible — operator can withdraw from hazard zone
analysis.plrP2Desc: avoidance not possible — insufficient time / awareness / exit access
analysis.plrFootnote: PLr is a suggestion based on Tab.K.1 ISO 13849-1:2023. Final verification is the responsibility of the safety engineer.
analysis.reflectionDesc: Before proceeding — answer honestly a few questions.
analysis.reflectionQ1: Have I seen this machine in operation and observed how operators use it?
analysis.reflectionQ2: Is every S and P value a conscious decision — not a system default?
analysis.reflectionQ3: Is the hazard list complete for this specific machine and its environment?
analysis.reflectionQ4: Do the protective measures I entered actually exist or will be implemented?
analysis.reflectionWarning: If the answer to any question is "no" or "I don't know" — consider going back.
analysis.confirmData: I confirm the accuracy of the data — this risk assessment was conducted in accordance with EN ISO 12100:2012.
analysis.unassignedWarning: {n} hazards without assigned protective measure level
analysis.neutralSP: ⚡ S and P values are your decisions — not automatic results
analysis.alertMissingSP: Define S and P values for {n} hazards. Assessment is the sole responsibility of the person conducting the analysis.
analysis.alertMissingJust: Complete S and P justifications for all hazards
analysis.alertMissingLC: Assign at least 1 lifecycle phase for each hazard
analysis.alertMissingPrepared: Complete "Prepared by" — name and surname are required.
analysis.alertMissingApproved: Complete "Approved by" — name and surname are required.
analysis.alertMissingPlrJust: Complete P1/P2 justification in PLr section for {n} hazards.
analysis.alertMissingConfirm: Check the confirmation checkbox before saving
analysis.credibilityHigh: High — analysis contains your own decisions
analysis.credibilityHint: 💡 Go back to step 3 and complete missing elements.
analysis.ceAttachments: 📎 Required attachments for CE technical documentation
analysis.ceAttachmentsDesc: According to Machinery Directive 2006/42/EC Annex VII
analysis.justificationSPlaceholder: e.g. risk of amputation on contact with spindle
analysis.justificationPPlaceholder: e.g. daily operator contact with hazard zone
analysis.plrP1Placeholder: e.g. zone with emergency exit, reaction time > 4s
analysis.plrP2Placeholder: e.g. enclosed zone, no audible warning

pdf.riskReportSub: Risk assessment in accordance with EN ISO 12100:2012
pdf.highConclusion: HIGH RISK — {{n}} hazards requiring immediate action
pdf.highConclusionText: Machine must not be put into operation without implementing protective measures for:
pdf.medConclusion: MEDIUM RISK — {{n}} hazards requiring technical measures
pdf.medConclusionText: Implementation of technical protective measures required before operation.
pdf.lowConclusionText: Acceptable when using PPE and following operating instructions.
pdf.method1: Elimination at source — design changes eliminating or limiting the hazard.
pdf.method2: Technical protective measures — guards, light curtains, safety mats, emergency stops.
pdf.method3: Information and training — ISO 7010 pictograms, work instructions, HSE training, PPE.
pdf.disclaimer: DISCLAIMER: This risk assessment is for guidance only. It does not replace consultation with a certified HSE specialist or official CE conformity assessment. Prepared in accordance with EN ISO 12100:2012 and Machinery Directive 2006/42/EC.
pdf.name: Name
pdf.ceNotifiedBodyNote: Complete only for machines listed in Annex IV of the Machinery Directive (e.g. circular saws, presses, industrial robots).
pdf.ceDeclaration: We hereby declare under our sole responsibility that the above described machinery complies with the provisions of the relevant directives and harmonised standards. Risk assessment performed in accordance with EN ISO 12100:2012
pdf.ceDeclarationEN: We hereby declare under our sole responsibility that the above described machinery complies with the provisions of the relevant Community directives and harmonised standards.
pdf.ceDisclaimer: This document requires data verification and signatures of authorised representatives before legal use. The manufacturer bears full responsibility for the correctness of the EC Declaration of Conformity.

profile.title: User Profile
profile.subtitle: Manage your account and company data
profile.name: Full name

admin.title: Admin Panel
admin.deleteConfirm: Delete user {{email}}? This action is irreversible.
admin.exportDesc: Download all system data in JSON format
